title: Xenium Slide Preparation
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7dinq","text":" FFPE-Tissue Preparation Guide (CG000578 Rev C, 10X Genomics)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4on9k","text":" ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a4hnv","text":"Xenium Analyzer User Guide (CG000584 Rev D and Rev E for Multi-modal Segmentation stained slides, 10X Genomics)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bnqc0","text":"Xenium Decoding Consumables Kit, (PN-1000487, 10X Genomics)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"25et1","text":"Xenium Autofluorescence Mix (PN-2000753, 10X Genomics)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44aod","text":"Xenium Nuclei Staining Buffer (PN-2000762, 10x Genomics)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":962811,"guid":"37875C04075611EF9C050A58A9FEAC02","name":"Xenium slides ","mol_weight":0,"sku":"3000941","url":"http://","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":613,"name":"10x Genomics","link":"https://www.10xgenomics.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Sections for Xenium were prepared according to the Xenium In Situ for FFPE-Tissue Preparation Guide (CG000578 Rev C, 10X Genomics) protocol.
Briefly, 5 µm sections were cut, floated in an RNAse-free water bath, and carefully placed onto the 10.45 mm x 22.45 mm sample area of Xenium slides (PN-3000941, 10X Genomics).

The slides were then placed on a drying rack at Room temperature for 00:30:00 to remove excess water. This was followed by a 03:00:00 incubation at 42 °C on a Xenium Thermocycler Adapter plate, positioned atop the 96-well block of a C1000 Touch Thermal Cycler (BioRad) with the lid open.
Subsequently, the slides were stored Overnight at Room temperature with a desiccant to facilitate further drying.
The slides were then processed following the Xenium In Situ for FFPE Deparaffinization and Decrosslinking protocol (CG000580 Rev C, 10X Genomics).
In brief, the slides were placed again on a Xenium Thermocycler Adapter plate atop the 96-well block of a thermocycler with the lid open, and incubated at 60 °C for 02:00:00 .

They were then equilibrated to Room temperature for 00:07:00 , deparaffinized, and rehydrated by immersion twice in xylene for 10 minutes each, twice in 100% ethanol for 10 minutes each, twice in 96% ethanol for 10 minutes each, once in 70% ethanol for 00:03:00 , and once in water for 00:00:20 .
Deparaffinized, and rehydrated by immersion in xylene for 00:10:00 (1/2).
Deparaffinized, and rehydrated by immersion in xylene for 00:10:00 (2/2).
Deparaffinized, and rehydrated by immersion in 100% ethanol for 00:10:00 (1/2).
Deparaffinized, and rehydrated by immersion in 100% ethanol for 00:10:00 (2/2).
Deparaffinized, and rehydrated by immersion in 96% ethanol for 00:10:00 (1/2).
Deparaffinized, and rehydrated by immersion in 96% ethanol for 00:10:00 (2/2).
Subsequently, the Xenium slides were assembled into Xenium cassettes and hydrated with 1X PBS.
The tissue sections were reverse crosslinked using a decrosslinking buffer containing tissue enhancer, urea, and perm enzyme B, at 80 °C for 00:30:00 , followed by three washes with PBS-T.
Slides were then immediately processed following the Xenium In Situ Gene Expression user guide (CG000582 Rev E, 10X Genomics) for the remaining slide preparation steps.

Briefly, the pre-designed gene expression probe set, Xenium Human Multi-Tissue and Cancer Panel (10X-1000626, 10X Genomics) targeting 377 human genes, was utilized for prostate cancer, ileum, appendix, pancreas, and colon samples. For breast cancer samples, the Xenium Human Breast Panel (10X-1000463, 10X Genomics) targeting 380 human genes was used.

Panels were denatured at 95 °C for 00:02:00 , crash-cool On ice for 00:01:00 , and then equilibrated to Room temperature before being added to the probe hybridization buffer and TE buffer to make the probe hybridization mix.
The slides were incubated with the hybridization mix at 50 °C for 17:00:00 .
The slides were washed three times with PBS-T for 1 minute each then incubated with a post-hybridisation wash buffer for 00:30:00 at 37 °C .
The slides were washed with PBS-T for 00:01:00 (1/3).
The slides were washed with PBS-T for 00:01:00 (2/3).
The slides were washed with PBS-T for 00:01:00 (3/3).
The slides were washed three times with PBS-T for 1 minute each and incubated with ligation mix for 02:00:00 at 37 °C .
The slides were washed the slides with PBS-T for 00:01:00 (1/3).
The slides were washed the slides with PBS-T for 00:01:00 (2/3).
The slides were washed the slides with PBS-T for 00:01:00 (3/3).
The slides were then assembled into Xenium Cassettes and loaded into the Xenium Analyzer for imaging and decoding.

Stain was then enhanced by the addition of Xenium Staining Enhancer reagent, followed by treatment of the sections with Xenium Autofluorescence Mix (PN-2000753, 10X Genomics) to diminish unwanted autofluorescence and enhance the signal-to-noise ratio in the tissue sections.

Subsequently, Xenium Nuclei Staining Buffer (PN-2000762, 10x Genomics) was used to help the identification of tissues or regions of interest during the instrument's overview scan. These procedures were uniformly followed at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, Weill Cornell Medical Center, 10x Genomics, and the University of Adelaide, ensuring consistency in the fields of view (FOVs) across all four locations. Breast cancer samples processed as part of the 10x Genomics’ Catalyst Program followed the methods described in Reference https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-023-43458-x; these experiments were carried out at 10X Genomics headquarters (Pleasanton, CA). Sections from the prostate cancer samples were also subjected to Xenium Multimodal segmentation through an early access program and processed at the 10X Genomics headquarters (Pleasanton, CA). In brief, the procedure for in situ gene expression with cell segmentation staining was conducted following the Demonstrated Protocol for Xenium In Situ for FFPE - Tissue Preparation Guide (CG000578 Rev C, 10X Genomics), the Xenium In Situ Gene Expression User Guide (CG000582 Rev F, 10X Genomics), and the Xenium In Situ Gene Expression Cell Segmentation User Guide (CG000749 Rev A, 10X Genomics). The combined protocol utilized the Xenium Human Multi-Tissue and Cancer Panel for in situ gene expression and the Xenium Cell Segmentation Staining Reagents add-on (PN-1000661, 10X Genomics) for segmentation staining. This add-on kit contains a single cocktail of Xenium Multi-Tissue Stain Mix (PN-2000991, 10X Genomics), which contains a cocktail of antibodies labeling the membranes (anti-ATP1A1/CD45/E-cadherin), antibodies labeling the cell interior (anti-alphaSMA/Vimentin) and a universal interior label against Ribosomal RNA (18S rRNA).

Slides were then washed three times with TE buffer then three washes with 1X PBS for 1 minute each followed by incubation with reducing agent B for 00:10:00 , washed once in 70% ethanol for 00:01:00 , twice in 100% ethanol then treated with autofluorescence solution for 10 minutes in the dark.
The slides were washed three times with 100% ethanol for 2 minutes each then dry for 00:05:00 at 37 °C before rehydrating with 1X PBS then PBS-T for 00:02:00 .
Three 1-minute PBS-T washes were followed by a 02:00:00 incubation at 30 °C with an amplification mix.
Wash with 1X PBS for 00:01:00 (1/3).
Wash with 1X PBS for 00:01:00 (2/3).
Wash in 100% ethanol then treated with autofluorescence solution for 00:10:00 in the dark (1/2).
Wash in 100% ethanol then treated with autofluorescence solution for 00:10:00 in the dark (2/2).
Wash with 1X PBS for 00:01:00 (3/3).
Slides were stained with a nuclei staining buffer for 00:01:00 at Room temperature followed by three washes with PBS-T for 1 minute each in the dark.
The slides were washed with 100% ethanol for 00:02:00 (1/3).
The slides were washed with 100% ethanol for 00:02:00 (2/3).
The slides were washed with 100% ethanol for 00:02:00 (3/3).
Wash with PBS-T for 00:01:00 each in the dark (1/3).
Wash with PBS-T for 00:01:00 each in the dark (2/3).
Wash with PBS-T for 00:01:00 each in the dark (3/3).
creator: Spatial Touchstone
